  • Integrations

  • Privacy
Back to top
View this page

Executors¶

The executors module provides WASM and container runtime for executing validation checks.

Overview¶

Executors are versioned, portable validation modules that can be:

  • WASM modules: Sandboxed, fast startup, portable

  • Container images: Full-featured, flexible

┌─────────────┐     Arrow IPC      ┌─────────────┐
│   Engine    │ ─────────────────► │  Executor   │
│             │                    │  (WASM/     │
│             │ ◄───────────────── │  Container) │
└─────────────┘     CheckResult    └─────────────┘

ExecutorRuntime¶

Unified interface for both WASM and container execution.

from griot_core.executors import ExecutorRuntime

runtime = ExecutorRuntime()

# Check capabilities
caps = runtime.capabilities
print(f"WASM available: {caps.wasm_available}")
print(f"Container available: {caps.container_available}")

# Execute a check
result = await runtime.execute(executor_spec, check, arrow_data)

ExecutorRegistry¶

Registry of available executors.

from griot_core.executors import ExecutorRegistry

registry = ExecutorRegistry()

# Get built-in executor
spec = registry.get_executor("registry://executors/null-check@1.0")

# Register custom executor
registry.register_executor(my_executor_spec)

# List all executors
for spec in registry.list_executors():
    print(f"{spec.id}@{spec.version}: {spec.description}")

Built-in Executors¶

  • null-check: Check for null values

  • unique-check: Check for uniqueness

  • pattern-check: Regex pattern validation

  • range-check: Numeric range validation

  • row-count: Row count validation

  • freshness-check: Data freshness validation

  • masking-check: PII masking validation

  • pii-detection: Detect PII in data

  • distribution-drift: Statistical drift detection

  • referential-check: Referential integrity

WasmRuntime¶

WASM execution using wasmtime.

from griot_core.executors import WasmRuntime

runtime = WasmRuntime(cache_dir="/tmp/wasm-cache")

result = await runtime.execute(
    executor_spec,
    check,
    arrow_data,
    timeout=30,
)

ContainerRuntime¶

Container execution using Podman/Docker.

from griot_core.executors import ContainerRuntime, ContainerConfig

config = ContainerConfig(
    runtime="podman",  # or "docker"
    memory_limit="512m",
    cpu_limit="1.0",
    network_mode="none",  # Sandboxed
    timeout=60,
)

runtime = ContainerRuntime(config)

result = await runtime.execute(
    executor_spec,
    check,
    arrow_data,
)

ExecutorSpec¶

Specification for an executor.

@dataclass
class ExecutorSpec:
    id: str                    # e.g., "null-check"
    version: str               # e.g., "1.0.0"
    runtime: Runtime           # WASM or CONTAINER
    artifact_url: str          # URL to WASM file or OCI image
    description: str = ""
    input_schema: dict = None  # Expected parameters
    output_schema: dict = None # Result format
    tags: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)

CheckResult¶

Result from check execution.

@dataclass
class CheckResult:
    passed: bool
    metric_value: float | None = None
    threshold: float | None = None
    operator: str | None = None
    details: dict = field(default_factory=dict)
    samples: list[dict] = field(default_factory=list)
    error: str | None = None

API Reference¶

Griot Core Executor Runtime.

This module provides the executor runtime for running validation checks. Executors can be WASM modules or container images that receive Arrow IPC data and return CheckResult.

class griot_core.executors.CheckResult(passed, metric_value=None, threshold=None, operator=None, details=<factory>, samples=<factory>, error=None)[source]¶

Result of a check execution.

Returned by executors after validating data.

Parameters:

  • passed (bool)

  • metric_value (float | None)

  • threshold (float | None)

  • operator (str | None)

  • details (Dict[str, Any])

  • samples (List[Dict[str, Any]])

  • error (str | None)

passed¶

Whether the check passed

Type:

bool

metric_value¶

The computed metric value (e.g., null count, duplicate count)

Type:

float | None

threshold¶

The threshold that was applied

Type:

float | None

operator¶

The comparison operator used (e.g., “lte”, “gte”, “eq”)

Type:

str | None

details¶

Additional details about the check execution

Type:

Dict[str, Any]

samples¶

Sample rows that failed the check (for debugging)

Type:

List[Dict[str, Any]]

error¶

Error message if the check failed to execute

Type:

str | None

error: str | None = None¶
classmethod from_json(data)[source]¶

Parse a CheckResult from JSON bytes.

Parameters:

data (bytes)

Return type:

CheckResult

metric_value: float | None = None¶
operator: str | None = None¶
threshold: float | None = None¶
to_json()[source]¶

Serialize this CheckResult to JSON bytes.

Return type:

bytes

passed: bool¶
details: Dict[str, Any]¶
samples: List[Dict[str, Any]]¶
class griot_core.executors.ExecutorResult(check_result, executor_id, executor_version, execution_time_ms=None, memory_used_bytes=None, runtime=None)[source]¶

Full result from an executor invocation.

Includes the check result plus execution metadata.

Parameters:

  • check_result (CheckResult)

  • executor_id (str)

  • executor_version (str)

  • execution_time_ms (float | None)

  • memory_used_bytes (int | None)

  • runtime (Runtime | None)

check_result¶

The validation result

Type:

griot_core.executors.types.CheckResult

executor_id¶

ID of the executor that ran

Type:

str

executor_version¶

Version of the executor

Type:

str

execution_time_ms¶

How long execution took in milliseconds

Type:

float | None

memory_used_bytes¶

Memory used during execution

Type:

int | None

runtime¶

The runtime used (wasm or container)

Type:

griot_core.models.enums.Runtime | None

execution_time_ms: float | None = None¶
memory_used_bytes: int | None = None¶
runtime: Runtime | None = None¶
check_result: CheckResult¶
executor_id: str¶
executor_version: str¶
class griot_core.executors.ExecutorSpec(id, version, runtime, artifact_url, description='', input_schema=None, output_schema=None, tags=<factory>)[source]¶

Specification for an executor.

Executors are versioned, portable validation modules that can be either WASM modules or container images.

Parameters:

  • id (str)

  • version (str)

  • runtime (Runtime)

  • artifact_url (str)

  • description (str)

  • input_schema (Dict[str, Any] | None)

  • output_schema (Dict[str, Any] | None)

  • tags (List[str])

id¶

Unique identifier for this executor (e.g., “null-check”)

Type:

str

version¶

Semantic version (e.g., “1.0.0”)

Type:

str

runtime¶

The runtime type (wasm or container)

Type:

griot_core.models.enums.Runtime

artifact_url¶

URL to the executable artifact - WASM: URL to .wasm file - Container: OCI image reference

Type:

str

description¶

Human-readable description

Type:

str

input_schema¶

JSON Schema describing expected parameters

Type:

Dict[str, Any] | None

output_schema¶

JSON Schema describing the result format

Type:

Dict[str, Any] | None

tags¶

Tags for categorization

Type:

List[str]

description: str = ''¶
input_schema: Dict[str, Any] | None = None¶
output_schema: Dict[str, Any] | None = None¶
property uri: str¶

Get the registry URI for this executor.

id: str¶
version: str¶
runtime: Runtime¶
artifact_url: str¶
tags: List[str]¶
class griot_core.executors.ExecutorRegistry(cache_dir=None, fetchers=None, ttl_seconds=3600)[source]¶

Registry for managing and fetching executor specifications.

Supports multiple URI schemes: - registry://executors/null-check@1.0 -> Fetch from Griot registry - file://path/to/executor.wasm -> Local WASM file - oci://ghcr.io/griot/checks/drift:1.0 -> OCI container image

Example

>>> registry = ExecutorRegistry(cache_dir=Path("/tmp/executors"))
>>> spec = await registry.get_executor("registry://executors/null-check@1.0")
>>> print(spec.runtime)  # Runtime.WASM
Parameters:

  • cache_dir (Optional[Path])

  • fetchers (Optional[Dict[URIScheme, ExecutorFetcher]])

  • ttl_seconds (int)

BUILTIN_EXECUTORS: Dict[str, ExecutorSpec] = {'distribution-drift': ExecutorSpec(id='distribution-drift', version='1.0.0', runtime=<Runtime.CONTAINER: 'container'>, artifact_url='oci://ghcr.io/griot/executors/distribution-drift:1.0', description='Detect distribution drift using PSI/KL divergence', input_schema={'type': 'object', 'properties': {'column': {'type': 'string'}, 'baseline': {'type': 'object'}, 'method': {'type': 'string', 'enum': ['psi', 'kl']}, 'threshold': {'type': 'number'}}, 'required': ['column', 'method']}, output_schema=None, tags=['data-quality', 'drift']), 'freshness-check': ExecutorSpec(id='freshness-check', version='1.0.0', runtime=<Runtime.WASM: 'wasm'>, artifact_url='registry://executors/freshness-check@1.0/artifact.wasm', description='Check data freshness by timestamp column', input_schema={'type': 'object', 'properties': {'column': {'type': 'string'}, 'max_age': {'type': 'string'}}, 'required': ['column', 'max_age']}, output_schema=None, tags=['data-quality', 'freshness-check']), 'masking-check': ExecutorSpec(id='masking-check', version='1.0.0', runtime=<Runtime.WASM: 'wasm'>, artifact_url='registry://executors/masking-check@1.0/artifact.wasm', description='Verify PII masking is applied', input_schema={'type': 'object', 'properties': {'column': {'type': 'string'}, 'strategy': {'type': 'string'}, 'pii_type': {'type': 'string'}}, 'required': ['column']}, output_schema=None, tags=['privacy', 'masking-check']), 'null-check': ExecutorSpec(id='null-check', version='1.0.0', runtime=<Runtime.WASM: 'wasm'>, artifact_url='registry://executors/null-check@1.0/artifact.wasm', description='Count null values in a column', input_schema={'type': 'object', 'properties': {'column': {'type': 'string'}, 'threshold': {'type': 'number'}, 'operator': {'type': 'string', 'enum': ['lte', 'gte', 'eq', 'lt', 'gt']}}, 'required': ['column']}, output_schema=None, tags=['data-quality', 'null-check']), 'pattern-check': ExecutorSpec(id='pattern-check', version='1.0.0', runtime=<Runtime.WASM: 'wasm'>, artifact_url='registry://executors/pattern-check@1.0/artifact.wasm', description='Validate column values against a regex pattern', input_schema={'type': 'object', 'properties': {'column': {'type': 'string'}, 'pattern': {'type': 'string'}, 'threshold': {'type': 'number'}}, 'required': ['column', 'pattern']}, output_schema=None, tags=['data-quality', 'pattern-check']), 'pii-detection': ExecutorSpec(id='pii-detection', version='1.0.0', runtime=<Runtime.WASM: 'wasm'>, artifact_url='registry://executors/pii-detection@1.0/artifact.wasm', description='Detect undeclared PII in data', input_schema={'type': 'object', 'properties': {'column': {'type': 'string'}, 'patterns': {'type': 'array'}, 'declared_pii': {'type': 'boolean'}}, 'required': ['column']}, output_schema=None, tags=['privacy', 'pii-detection']), 'range-check': ExecutorSpec(id='range-check', version='1.0.0', runtime=<Runtime.WASM: 'wasm'>, artifact_url='registry://executors/range-check@1.0/artifact.wasm', description='Check values are within a min/max range', input_schema={'type': 'object', 'properties': {'column': {'type': 'string'}, 'min': {'type': 'number'}, 'max': {'type': 'number'}}, 'required': ['column']}, output_schema=None, tags=['data-quality', 'range-check']), 'referential-check': ExecutorSpec(id='referential-check', version='1.0.0', runtime=<Runtime.CONTAINER: 'container'>, artifact_url='oci://ghcr.io/griot/executors/referential-check:1.0', description='Check referential integrity between tables', input_schema={'type': 'object', 'properties': {'column': {'type': 'string'}, 'reference': {'type': 'object', 'properties': {'dataset': {'type': 'string'}, 'column': {'type': 'string'}}}}, 'required': ['column', 'reference']}, output_schema=None, tags=['data-quality', 'referential']), 'row-count': ExecutorSpec(id='row-count', version='1.0.0', runtime=<Runtime.WASM: 'wasm'>, artifact_url='registry://executors/row-count@1.0/artifact.wasm', description='Check row count is within bounds', input_schema={'type': 'object', 'properties': {'min': {'type': 'integer'}, 'max': {'type': 'integer'}}}, output_schema=None, tags=['data-quality', 'row-count']), 'unique-check': ExecutorSpec(id='unique-check', version='1.0.0', runtime=<Runtime.WASM: 'wasm'>, artifact_url='registry://executors/unique-check@1.0/artifact.wasm', description='Count duplicate values in a column', input_schema={'type': 'object', 'properties': {'column': {'type': 'string'}, 'threshold': {'type': 'number'}}, 'required': ['column']}, output_schema=None, tags=['data-quality', 'unique-check'])}¶
__init__(cache_dir=None, fetchers=None, ttl_seconds=3600)[source]¶

Initialize the executor registry.

Parameters:

  • cache_dir (Path | None) – Directory for caching executor artifacts

  • fetchers (Dict[URIScheme, ExecutorFetcher] | None) – Custom fetchers for different URI schemes

  • ttl_seconds (int) – Cache TTL in seconds (default 1 hour)

clear_cache()[source]¶

Clear the executor cache.

Return type:

None

get_cache_path(spec)[source]¶

Get the cache path for an executor artifact.

Parameters:

spec (ExecutorSpec)

Return type:

Path

async get_executor(uri)[source]¶

Get executor specification from URI.

Parameters:

uri (str) – Executor URI

Returns:

ExecutorSpec for the executor

Raises:

  • ExecutorNotFoundError – If executor cannot be found

  • InvalidExecutorURIError – If URI is invalid

Return type:

ExecutorSpec

list_executors()[source]¶

List all available executors.

Return type:

List[ExecutorSpec]

register_executor(spec)[source]¶

Register an executor specification.

Parameters:

spec (ExecutorSpec) – The executor specification to register

Return type:

None

exception griot_core.executors.ExecutorNotFoundError(uri, message='')[source]¶

Raised when an executor cannot be found.

Parameters:

  • uri (str)

  • message (str)

exception griot_core.executors.InvalidExecutorURIError(uri, reason='')[source]¶

Raised when an executor URI is invalid.

Parameters:

  • uri (str)

  • reason (str)

class griot_core.executors.WasmRuntime(cache_dir=None, memory_limit_mb=256, default_timeout=60)[source]¶

Runtime for executing WASM-based validation checks.

WASM modules receive Arrow IPC data and parameters, and return a CheckResult as JSON.

The WASM interface expects: - Input: Arrow IPC bytes + JSON parameters - Output: JSON-encoded CheckResult

Example

>>> runtime = WasmRuntime(cache_dir=Path("/tmp/wasm"))
>>> result = await runtime.execute(spec, check, arrow_data)
>>> print(result.check_result.passed)
Parameters:

  • cache_dir (Optional[Path])

  • memory_limit_mb (int)

  • default_timeout (int)

__init__(cache_dir=None, memory_limit_mb=256, default_timeout=60)[source]¶

Initialize the WASM runtime.

Parameters:

  • cache_dir (Path | None) – Directory for caching WASM modules

  • memory_limit_mb (int) – Default memory limit for WASM execution

  • default_timeout (int) – Default timeout in seconds

clear_cache()[source]¶

Clear the module cache.

Return type:

None

async execute(spec, check, arrow_data, timeout=None)[source]¶

Execute a WASM check.

Parameters:

  • spec (ExecutorSpec) – Executor specification

  • check (Check) – The check to execute

  • arrow_data (bytes) – Arrow IPC format data

  • timeout (int | None) – Optional timeout override

Returns:

WasmExecutionResult with the check result

Raises:

  • WasmModuleNotFoundError – If the module cannot be found

  • WasmExecutionError – If execution fails

Return type:

WasmExecutionResult

preload_module(spec)[source]¶

Preload a WASM module into the cache.

Parameters:

spec (ExecutorSpec)

Return type:

None

class griot_core.executors.WasmExecutionResult(check_result, execution_time_ms, memory_used_bytes=0, fuel_consumed=None)[source]¶

Result of WASM execution.

Parameters:

  • check_result (CheckResult)

  • execution_time_ms (float)

  • memory_used_bytes (int)

  • fuel_consumed (int | None)

fuel_consumed: int | None = None¶
memory_used_bytes: int = 0¶
check_result: CheckResult¶
execution_time_ms: float¶
class griot_core.executors.ContainerRuntime(config=None)[source]¶

Runtime for executing container-based validation checks.

Containers receive Arrow data via a mounted volume and parameters via environment variables. Results are returned via stdout.

The container interface expects: - Input: Arrow IPC file at /data/input.arrow - Parameters: PARAMETERS environment variable (JSON) - Output: JSON-encoded CheckResult to stdout

Example

>>> runtime = ContainerRuntime(config=ContainerConfig(runtime="docker"))
>>> result = await runtime.execute(spec, check, arrow_data)
>>> print(result.check_result.passed)
Parameters:

config (Optional[ContainerConfig])

__init__(config=None)[source]¶

Initialize the container runtime.

Parameters:

config (ContainerConfig | None) – Container execution configuration

async execute(spec, check, arrow_data, timeout=None)[source]¶

Execute a container check.

Parameters:

  • spec (ExecutorSpec) – Executor specification

  • check (Check) – The check to execute

  • arrow_data (bytes) – Arrow IPC format data

  • timeout (int | None) – Optional timeout override

Returns:

ContainerExecutionResult with the check result

Raises:

  • ContainerRuntimeNotFoundError – If no container runtime

  • ContainerExecutionError – If execution fails

Return type:

ContainerExecutionResult

get_runtime_info()[source]¶

Get information about the container runtime.

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]

is_available()[source]¶

Check if the container runtime is available.

Return type:

bool

async pull_image(image)[source]¶

Pull a container image.

Parameters:

image (str) – Image reference to pull

Returns:

True if successful

Return type:

bool

class griot_core.executors.ContainerConfig(runtime='podman', network_mode='none', memory_limit='512m', cpu_limit=1.0, timeout_seconds=300, pull_policy='if-not-present')[source]¶

Configuration for container execution.

Parameters:

  • runtime (str)

  • network_mode (str)

  • memory_limit (str)

  • cpu_limit (float)

  • timeout_seconds (int)

  • pull_policy (str)

cpu_limit: float = 1.0¶
memory_limit: str = '512m'¶
network_mode: str = 'none'¶
pull_policy: str = 'if-not-present'¶
runtime: str = 'podman'¶
timeout_seconds: int = 300¶
class griot_core.executors.ContainerExecutionResult(check_result, execution_time_ms, container_id=None, exit_code=0, stdout='', stderr='')[source]¶

Result of container execution.

Parameters:

  • check_result (CheckResult)

  • execution_time_ms (float)

  • container_id (str | None)

  • exit_code (int)

  • stdout (str)

  • stderr (str)

container_id: str | None = None¶
exit_code: int = 0¶
stderr: str = ''¶
stdout: str = ''¶
check_result: CheckResult¶
execution_time_ms: float¶
class griot_core.executors.ExecutorRuntime(wasm_cache_dir=None, container_config=None, runtime_preference=None)[source]¶

Unified executor runtime that handles both WASM and Container execution.

Automatically selects the appropriate runtime based on the executor specification and the configured runtime preferences.

Example

>>> runtime = ExecutorRuntime(
...     wasm_cache_dir=Path("/tmp/wasm"),
...     container_config=ContainerConfig(runtime="docker")
... )
>>> result = await runtime.execute(spec, check, arrow_data)
>>> print(result.check_result.passed)
Parameters:

  • wasm_cache_dir (Optional[Path])

  • container_config (Optional[ContainerConfig])

  • runtime_preference (Optional[List[Runtime]])

__init__(wasm_cache_dir=None, container_config=None, runtime_preference=None)[source]¶

Initialize the unified executor runtime.

Parameters:

  • wasm_cache_dir (Path | None) – Cache directory for WASM modules

  • container_config (ContainerConfig | None) – Configuration for container execution

  • runtime_preference (List[Runtime] | None) – Preferred runtime order (default: [WASM, CONTAINER])

clear_caches()[source]¶

Clear all runtime caches.

Return type:

None

async execute(spec, check, arrow_data, runtime_preference=None, timeout=None)[source]¶

Execute a check using the appropriate runtime.

Parameters:

  • spec (ExecutorSpec) – Executor specification

  • check (Check) – The check to execute

  • arrow_data (bytes) – Arrow IPC format data

  • runtime_preference (List[Runtime] | None) – Override runtime preference for this execution

  • timeout (int | None) – Optional timeout in seconds

Returns:

ExecutorResult with the check result and execution metadata

Return type:

ExecutorResult

get_capabilities()[source]¶

Get the capabilities of this runtime environment.

Return type:

RuntimeCapabilities

is_runtime_available(runtime)[source]¶

Check if a specific runtime is available.

Parameters:

runtime (Runtime)

Return type:

bool

async preload_executors(specs)[source]¶

Preload executors to warm up caches.

Parameters:

specs (List[ExecutorSpec]) – List of executor specifications to preload

Returns:

Dictionary mapping executor IDs to preload success status

Return type:

Dict[str, bool]

class griot_core.executors.RuntimeCapabilities(wasm_available, container_available, container_runtime=None)[source]¶

Capabilities of the runtime environment.

Parameters:

  • wasm_available (bool)

  • container_available (bool)

  • container_runtime (str | None)

container_runtime: str | None = None¶
wasm_available: bool¶
container_available: bool¶

Executor types and result models.

This module defines the types used by the executor runtime, including ExecutorSpec for describing executors and CheckResult for validation results.

class griot_core.executors.types.ExecutorSpec(id, version, runtime, artifact_url, description='', input_schema=None, output_schema=None, tags=<factory>)[source]¶

Specification for an executor.

Executors are versioned, portable validation modules that can be either WASM modules or container images.

Parameters:

  • id (str)

  • version (str)

  • runtime (Runtime)

  • artifact_url (str)

  • description (str)

  • input_schema (Dict[str, Any] | None)

  • output_schema (Dict[str, Any] | None)

  • tags (List[str])

id¶

Unique identifier for this executor (e.g., “null-check”)

Type:

str

version¶

Semantic version (e.g., “1.0.0”)

Type:

str

runtime¶

The runtime type (wasm or container)

Type:

griot_core.models.enums.Runtime

artifact_url¶

URL to the executable artifact - WASM: URL to .wasm file - Container: OCI image reference

Type:

str

description¶

Human-readable description

Type:

str

input_schema¶

JSON Schema describing expected parameters

Type:

Dict[str, Any] | None

output_schema¶

JSON Schema describing the result format

Type:

Dict[str, Any] | None

tags¶

Tags for categorization

Type:

List[str]

property uri: str¶

Get the registry URI for this executor.

class griot_core.executors.types.CheckResult(passed, metric_value=None, threshold=None, operator=None, details=<factory>, samples=<factory>, error=None)[source]¶

Result of a check execution.

Returned by executors after validating data.

Parameters:

  • passed (bool)

  • metric_value (float | None)

  • threshold (float | None)

  • operator (str | None)

  • details (Dict[str, Any])

  • samples (List[Dict[str, Any]])

  • error (str | None)

passed¶

Whether the check passed

Type:

bool

metric_value¶

The computed metric value (e.g., null count, duplicate count)

Type:

float | None

threshold¶

The threshold that was applied

Type:

float | None

operator¶

The comparison operator used (e.g., “lte”, “gte”, “eq”)

Type:

str | None

details¶

Additional details about the check execution

Type:

Dict[str, Any]

samples¶

Sample rows that failed the check (for debugging)

Type:

List[Dict[str, Any]]

error¶

Error message if the check failed to execute

Type:

str | None

classmethod from_json(data)[source]¶

Parse a CheckResult from JSON bytes.

Parameters:

data (bytes)

Return type:

CheckResult

to_json()[source]¶

Serialize this CheckResult to JSON bytes.

Return type:

bytes

class griot_core.executors.types.ExecutorResult(check_result, executor_id, executor_version, execution_time_ms=None, memory_used_bytes=None, runtime=None)[source]¶

Full result from an executor invocation.

Includes the check result plus execution metadata.

Parameters:

  • check_result (CheckResult)

  • executor_id (str)

  • executor_version (str)

  • execution_time_ms (float | None)

  • memory_used_bytes (int | None)

  • runtime (Runtime | None)

check_result¶

The validation result

Type:

griot_core.executors.types.CheckResult

executor_id¶

ID of the executor that ran

Type:

str

executor_version¶

Version of the executor

Type:

str

execution_time_ms¶

How long execution took in milliseconds

Type:

float | None

memory_used_bytes¶

Memory used during execution

Type:

int | None

runtime¶

The runtime used (wasm or container)

Type:

griot_core.models.enums.Runtime | None

Executor registry for fetching executor specifications.

The registry manages executor specifications and provides caching for efficient executor lookups.

class griot_core.executors.registry.URIScheme(*values)[source]¶

Supported URI schemes for executor references.

class griot_core.executors.registry.ExecutorManifest(id, version, runtime, artifact_path, entry_point='validate', description='', input_schema=None, output_schema=None, dependencies=<factory>, tags=<factory>, checksum=None)[source]¶

Manifest describing an executor package.

Contains metadata about the executor and how to run it.

Parameters:

  • id (str)

  • version (str)

  • runtime (Runtime)

  • artifact_path (str)

  • entry_point (str)

  • description (str)

  • input_schema (Dict[str, Any] | None)

  • output_schema (Dict[str, Any] | None)

  • dependencies (List[str])

  • tags (List[str])

  • checksum (str | None)

class griot_core.executors.registry.ExecutorFetcher(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Protocol for fetching executor artifacts.

async fetch_spec(uri)[source]¶

Fetch executor specification from URI.

Parameters:

uri (str)

Return type:

ExecutorSpec

async fetch_artifact(spec)[source]¶

Fetch the actual executor artifact (WASM or container reference).

Parameters:

spec (ExecutorSpec)

Return type:

bytes

exception griot_core.executors.registry.ExecutorNotFoundError(uri, message='')[source]¶

Raised when an executor cannot be found.

Parameters:

  • uri (str)

  • message (str)

exception griot_core.executors.registry.InvalidExecutorURIError(uri, reason='')[source]¶

Raised when an executor URI is invalid.

Parameters:

  • uri (str)

  • reason (str)

class griot_core.executors.registry.CachedExecutor(spec, artifact_path=None, fetched_at=None, checksum=None)[source]¶

Cached executor with metadata.

Parameters:

  • spec (ExecutorSpec)

  • artifact_path (Path | None)

  • fetched_at (float | None)

  • checksum (str | None)

class griot_core.executors.registry.ExecutorRegistry(cache_dir=None, fetchers=None, ttl_seconds=3600)[source]¶

Registry for managing and fetching executor specifications.

Supports multiple URI schemes: - registry://executors/null-check@1.0 -> Fetch from Griot registry - file://path/to/executor.wasm -> Local WASM file - oci://ghcr.io/griot/checks/drift:1.0 -> OCI container image

Example

>>> registry = ExecutorRegistry(cache_dir=Path("/tmp/executors"))
>>> spec = await registry.get_executor("registry://executors/null-check@1.0")
>>> print(spec.runtime)  # Runtime.WASM
Parameters:

  • cache_dir (Optional[Path])

  • fetchers (Optional[Dict[URIScheme, ExecutorFetcher]])

  • ttl_seconds (int)

__init__(cache_dir=None, fetchers=None, ttl_seconds=3600)[source]¶

Initialize the executor registry.

Parameters:

  • cache_dir (Path | None) – Directory for caching executor artifacts

  • fetchers (Dict[URIScheme, ExecutorFetcher] | None) – Custom fetchers for different URI schemes

  • ttl_seconds (int) – Cache TTL in seconds (default 1 hour)

async get_executor(uri)[source]¶

Get executor specification from URI.

Parameters:

uri (str) – Executor URI

Returns:

ExecutorSpec for the executor

Raises:

  • ExecutorNotFoundError – If executor cannot be found

  • InvalidExecutorURIError – If URI is invalid

Return type:

ExecutorSpec

register_executor(spec)[source]¶

Register an executor specification.

Parameters:

spec (ExecutorSpec) – The executor specification to register

Return type:

None

list_executors()[source]¶

List all available executors.

Return type:

List[ExecutorSpec]

clear_cache()[source]¶

Clear the executor cache.

Return type:

None

get_cache_path(spec)[source]¶

Get the cache path for an executor artifact.

Parameters:

spec (ExecutorSpec)

Return type:

Path

Unified executor runtime.

Combines WASM and Container runtimes into a single interface that automatically selects the appropriate runtime based on executor specification and preferences.

class griot_core.executors.runtime.RuntimeCapabilities(wasm_available, container_available, container_runtime=None)[source]¶

Capabilities of the runtime environment.

Parameters:

  • wasm_available (bool)

  • container_available (bool)

  • container_runtime (str | None)

class griot_core.executors.runtime.ExecutorRuntime(wasm_cache_dir=None, container_config=None, runtime_preference=None)[source]¶

Unified executor runtime that handles both WASM and Container execution.

Automatically selects the appropriate runtime based on the executor specification and the configured runtime preferences.

Example

>>> runtime = ExecutorRuntime(
...     wasm_cache_dir=Path("/tmp/wasm"),
...     container_config=ContainerConfig(runtime="docker")
... )
>>> result = await runtime.execute(spec, check, arrow_data)
>>> print(result.check_result.passed)
Parameters:

  • wasm_cache_dir (Optional[Path])

  • container_config (Optional[ContainerConfig])

  • runtime_preference (Optional[List[Runtime]])

__init__(wasm_cache_dir=None, container_config=None, runtime_preference=None)[source]¶

Initialize the unified executor runtime.

Parameters:

  • wasm_cache_dir (Path | None) – Cache directory for WASM modules

  • container_config (ContainerConfig | None) – Configuration for container execution

  • runtime_preference (List[Runtime] | None) – Preferred runtime order (default: [WASM, CONTAINER])

async execute(spec, check, arrow_data, runtime_preference=None, timeout=None)[source]¶

Execute a check using the appropriate runtime.

Parameters:

  • spec (ExecutorSpec) – Executor specification

  • check (Check) – The check to execute

  • arrow_data (bytes) – Arrow IPC format data

  • runtime_preference (List[Runtime] | None) – Override runtime preference for this execution

  • timeout (int | None) – Optional timeout in seconds

Returns:

ExecutorResult with the check result and execution metadata

Return type:

ExecutorResult

get_capabilities()[source]¶

Get the capabilities of this runtime environment.

Return type:

RuntimeCapabilities

is_runtime_available(runtime)[source]¶

Check if a specific runtime is available.

Parameters:

runtime (Runtime)

Return type:

bool

async preload_executors(specs)[source]¶

Preload executors to warm up caches.

Parameters:

specs (List[ExecutorSpec]) – List of executor specifications to preload

Returns:

Dictionary mapping executor IDs to preload success status

Return type:

Dict[str, bool]

clear_caches()[source]¶

Clear all runtime caches.

Return type:

None

WASM runtime for executing WASM-based checks.

Uses wasmtime to execute WASM modules that receive Arrow IPC data and return CheckResult.

class griot_core.executors.wasm_runtime.WasmExecutionContext(module_path, memory_limit_mb=256, timeout_seconds=60, fuel_limit=None)[source]¶

Context for WASM execution.

Parameters:

  • module_path (Path)

  • memory_limit_mb (int)

  • timeout_seconds (int)

  • fuel_limit (int | None)

class griot_core.executors.wasm_runtime.WasmExecutionResult(check_result, execution_time_ms, memory_used_bytes=0, fuel_consumed=None)[source]¶

Result of WASM execution.

Parameters:

  • check_result (CheckResult)

  • execution_time_ms (float)

  • memory_used_bytes (int)

  • fuel_consumed (int | None)

exception griot_core.executors.wasm_runtime.WasmModuleNotFoundError[source]¶

Raised when a WASM module cannot be found.

exception griot_core.executors.wasm_runtime.WasmExecutionError[source]¶

Raised when WASM execution fails.

class griot_core.executors.wasm_runtime.WasmRuntime(cache_dir=None, memory_limit_mb=256, default_timeout=60)[source]¶

Runtime for executing WASM-based validation checks.

WASM modules receive Arrow IPC data and parameters, and return a CheckResult as JSON.

The WASM interface expects: - Input: Arrow IPC bytes + JSON parameters - Output: JSON-encoded CheckResult

Example

>>> runtime = WasmRuntime(cache_dir=Path("/tmp/wasm"))
>>> result = await runtime.execute(spec, check, arrow_data)
>>> print(result.check_result.passed)
Parameters:

  • cache_dir (Optional[Path])

  • memory_limit_mb (int)

  • default_timeout (int)

__init__(cache_dir=None, memory_limit_mb=256, default_timeout=60)[source]¶

Initialize the WASM runtime.

Parameters:

  • cache_dir (Path | None) – Directory for caching WASM modules

  • memory_limit_mb (int) – Default memory limit for WASM execution

  • default_timeout (int) – Default timeout in seconds

async execute(spec, check, arrow_data, timeout=None)[source]¶

Execute a WASM check.

Parameters:

  • spec (ExecutorSpec) – Executor specification

  • check (Check) – The check to execute

  • arrow_data (bytes) – Arrow IPC format data

  • timeout (int | None) – Optional timeout override

Returns:

WasmExecutionResult with the check result

Raises:

  • WasmModuleNotFoundError – If the module cannot be found

  • WasmExecutionError – If execution fails

Return type:

WasmExecutionResult

clear_cache()[source]¶

Clear the module cache.

Return type:

None

preload_module(spec)[source]¶

Preload a WASM module into the cache.

Parameters:

spec (ExecutorSpec)

Return type:

None

Container runtime for executing container-based checks.

Uses Podman or Docker to execute container images that receive Arrow IPC data and return CheckResult.

class griot_core.executors.container_runtime.ContainerConfig(runtime='podman', network_mode='none', memory_limit='512m', cpu_limit=1.0, timeout_seconds=300, pull_policy='if-not-present')[source]¶

Configuration for container execution.

Parameters:

  • runtime (str)

  • network_mode (str)

  • memory_limit (str)

  • cpu_limit (float)

  • timeout_seconds (int)

  • pull_policy (str)

class griot_core.executors.container_runtime.ContainerExecutionResult(check_result, execution_time_ms, container_id=None, exit_code=0, stdout='', stderr='')[source]¶

Result of container execution.

Parameters:

  • check_result (CheckResult)

  • execution_time_ms (float)

  • container_id (str | None)

  • exit_code (int)

  • stdout (str)

  • stderr (str)

exception griot_core.executors.container_runtime.ContainerRuntimeNotFoundError[source]¶

Raised when container runtime is not available.

exception griot_core.executors.container_runtime.ContainerExecutionError[source]¶

Raised when container execution fails.

exception griot_core.executors.container_runtime.ContainerImageNotFoundError[source]¶

Raised when a container image cannot be found.

class griot_core.executors.container_runtime.ContainerRuntime(config=None)[source]¶

Runtime for executing container-based validation checks.

Containers receive Arrow data via a mounted volume and parameters via environment variables. Results are returned via stdout.

The container interface expects: - Input: Arrow IPC file at /data/input.arrow - Parameters: PARAMETERS environment variable (JSON) - Output: JSON-encoded CheckResult to stdout

Example

>>> runtime = ContainerRuntime(config=ContainerConfig(runtime="docker"))
>>> result = await runtime.execute(spec, check, arrow_data)
>>> print(result.check_result.passed)
Parameters:

config (Optional[ContainerConfig])

__init__(config=None)[source]¶

Initialize the container runtime.

Parameters:

config (ContainerConfig | None) – Container execution configuration

async execute(spec, check, arrow_data, timeout=None)[source]¶

Execute a container check.

Parameters:

  • spec (ExecutorSpec) – Executor specification

  • check (Check) – The check to execute

  • arrow_data (bytes) – Arrow IPC format data

  • timeout (int | None) – Optional timeout override

Returns:

ContainerExecutionResult with the check result

Raises:

  • ContainerRuntimeNotFoundError – If no container runtime

  • ContainerExecutionError – If execution fails

Return type:

ContainerExecutionResult

async pull_image(image)[source]¶

Pull a container image.

Parameters:

image (str) – Image reference to pull

Returns:

True if successful

Return type:

bool

is_available()[source]¶

Check if the container runtime is available.

Return type:

bool

get_runtime_info()[source]¶

Get information about the container runtime.

Return type:

Dict[str, Any]
